mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing equity markets to price in lower earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and aggregate retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth that outpaces supply constraints, driving corporate revenue acceleration and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observed quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity utilization exhaustion and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive or flat 20-day equity returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal direction reverses when tested against early-cycle data.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 2.0 percent signals a restrictive monetary stance that increases discount rates and reduces risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 55% of observed months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings for materials and consumer discretionary firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases show no significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 105, manufacturing capacity operates above historical baselines, signaling robust supply-side momentum that supports corporate output and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 105.
